title: Corymborkis brevistylis
taxonomy: Corymborkis brevistylis is classified as a species within the genus Corymborkis, which is a group of orchids, and further classified as a member of the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the order Asparagales and the clade monocots. The family Orchidaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, often referred to as orchids. Within the Orchidaceae family, Corymborkis brevistylis is further grouped as a member of the subfamily Cranichideae. This classification is based on morphological and genetic characteristics that distinguish it from other orchid species.

intro: Corymborkis brevistylis, also known as the Coralroot, is a type of orchid that belongs to the genus Corymborkis. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Corymborkis brevistylis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
